points: Living beings are distinguished by seven primary features: specialized movement, growth over time, nutrition, respiration, excretion, response to stimuli, and reproduction.
Reproduction is essential for the continuity of life beyond the death of an individual organism.
Response to stimulus (e.g., light, touch, heat) is a fundamental ability of all living organisms to react to environmental changes.
Germination of seeds requires a specific balance of air and water; sunlight is generally not essential for the initial sprouting phase.
Plant shoots exhibit positive phototropism (growing toward light), while roots always grow downward into the soil.
The life cycle of a plant follows a progression from seed germination through maturation, flowering, and seed production.
Mosquitoes undergo a four-stage life cycle: Egg, Larva, Pupa, and Adult; disrupting any stage (e.g., using kerosene in stagnant water) stops their breeding.
Frogs experience metamorphosis starting from aquatic spawn (eggs) and tadpoles to semi-aquatic froglets and finally land-dwelling adult frogs.
Continuous biological processes like growth, reproduction, and eventual death define the living world and maintain its flourishing cycle.
